description:
secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1378-1385: When the spawned scoped thread panics, the panic payload (Box<dyn Any + Send>) is discarded by 'unwrap_or_else(|_| ...)' and replaced with the static string 'client init thread panicked'. The original panic message is lost.

Consequence: when a backend's async init panics inside run_on_new_thread, the operator sees only 'backend `X` error: client init thread panicked' with no clue what panicked. Diagnostics on a production crash become an issue-report ping-pong.

Same issue at lib.rs:1447-1452 (get_blocking inside-runtime path) — message there is 'get_blocking thread panicked', equally opaque.

Fix: extract the panic payload and downcast to &str / String to recover the message. std::panic::catch_unwind / thread::Result::unwrap_err lets you call .downcast_ref::<&str>() and .downcast_ref::<String>(); fall back to a fixed string only if neither matches.
title: run_on_new_thread discards inner-thread panic message
